{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} Massha | founded= Before Enryaku 23 (804) | main_hall= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} | alternative_names= | pilgrimage_sites= | annual_festival= | rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} }} '''Kamoshimo Shrine''' (鴨下神社, かもしもじんじゃ) is a **massha** (末社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. The **three enshrined deities** are considered **water deities** of the local region.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 59Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 68. == Overview == Kamoshimo Shrine is located in {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is one of **13 massha** of the **Inner Shrine (Naikū) located in Tamaki Town** and holds the **highest Rank** among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 13–14 Approximately **2.5 km away** from Kamoshimo Shrine is {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|ja|鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}}, an **Inner Shrine sessha** (摂社), which enshrines the **same deities** and shares a connection with Kamoshimo Shrine, as both are located in **Shirota-no-go (城田郷)**.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p. 200. The name **"Kamo"** was traditionally believed to derive from an **old place name**. However, with the excavation of the **Saikū Site (斎宮遺跡)**, a connection to the **Kamo clan (賀茂氏)**, who served as **water administrators (水司)**, has been proposed.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p. 199. The name **"Shimo" (下, "lower")** refers to its position at the **foot of a mountain**, in contrast to **Kamo Shrine**, which is located **midway up the mountain**.. The shrine grounds cover **3 tan, 9 se, 14 bu (approximately 3,914 m²)**Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p. 13, surrounded by **grape vineyards**. The shrine hall is built in **Shinmei-zukuri (神明造)** style with **ita-buki (板葺, plank roofing)**, enclosed by a **single-layered tamagaki fence (玉垣)**. There is **no offertory box (賽銭箱)**. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: - **Ishi-Korowake-no-Mikoto (石己呂和居命)** - **Kamo-Hiko-no-Mikoto (鴨比古命)** - **Kamo-Hime-no-Mikoto (鴨比賣命)** All three deities are considered **guardian deities of water management and irrigation in Katsuta (勝田)**. - **Ishi-Korowake-no-Mikoto** is the **child of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命)**, the **same deity enshrined at Kamo Shrine**. - **Kamo-Hiko-no-Mikoto and Kamo-Hime-no-Mikoto** are **male and female deities** whose names derive from the **common noun "Kamo" (鴨, "duck")**, symbolizing their dual nature. == History == Kamoshimo Shrine is a **Shikigai-sha (式外社)**, meaning it is **not listed in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō (延喜式神名帳)**. However, since it is classified as an **Ise Grand Shrine massha**, it must have existed before the **compilation of the Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō (皇大神宮儀式帳)**, that is, before **Enryaku 23 (804)**.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22 Like Kamo Shrine, Kamoshimo Shrine was **originally regulated to follow the same 20-year rebuilding cycle** (*shikinen sengū*, 神宮式年遷宮) as the **Ise Grand Shrine**, overseen by the **Zōkyōshi (造宮使)**. During **ancient times**, Kamoshimo Shrine was categorized as a **"Taisha" (田社, "Rice Paddy Shrine")**. However, it was the **only Taisha shrine** to receive such **special treatment**. However, due to the **political turmoil of the Sengoku period (戦国時代)**, the shrine fell into neglect and was left in **a state of disrepair**.Kaneko (1983): p. 61. In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was **restored** along with {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}}.Sakurai (1991): pp. 280–281. == Access == === Public Transportation === - **From Tamaki Station (田丸駅) on the JR Sangu Line (参宮線)** ** Approximately **24 minutes on foot (2.0 km southwest)** === By Car === - **From Tamaki IC (玉城IC) on the Ise Expressway (伊勢自動車道)** ** Take **Mie Prefectural Route 65 (三重県道65号度会玉城線, Sunny Road)** north for approximately **3 minutes (1.3 km)** ** **No designated parking lot available** == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * Ise Bunka-sha, ed. *O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage* (お伊勢さん125社めぐり), Ise Bunka-sha, December 23, 2008, 151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4. * Ujiyamada City Hall, ed. *Ujiyamada City History, Vol. 1* (宇治山田市史 上巻), Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, 1929, 862p. * Gakken Publishing, *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine* (伊勢神宮に行こう), Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by Sonoda Minoru, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1. * Kaneko, Nobuo. *Tamaki Town History, Vol. 1: The History and Legends of Southern Ise* (玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―), Mie Prefecture Historical Materials Series No. 61, Mie Prefecture Historical Society, August 10, 1983, 276p. * Sakurai, Katsunoshin. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine* (伊勢神宮の祖型と展開), Kokusho Publishing, November 30, 1991, 318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3. * Shikinaisha Research Group, ed. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6: Tōkaidō 1* (式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1), Kōgakkan University Press, February 28, 1990, 690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8. {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Massha of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Kamoshimo Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ise Province]] [[Category:Massha and Sessha]] [[Category:Shikigai-sha]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:Buildings in Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:鴨下神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:式外社]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamoshimo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:鴨下神社]] [[sn:Kamoshimo Shrine]] [[sn:amoshimo Shrine]]

{{Shrine | name= Kamoshimo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image=[[File:Kamoshimo-jinja (Naiku) 03.jpg|270px|Kamoshimo Shrine]] | location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町|12=draft|12=draft}} Katsuta Aza Uenoyama 3642 | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 28 | lat_seconds = 44.8 | longitude = 136 | lon_minutes = 37 | lon_seconds = 16.3 | map= Japan Mie | enshrined_deities= Ishi-Korowake-no-Mikoto (石己呂和居命)
Kamo-Hiko-no-Mikoto (鴨比古命)
Kamo-Hime-no-Mikoto (鴨比賣命) | shrine_Rank= {{ill|Shikigai-sha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} Massha | founded= Before Enryaku 23 (804) | main_hall= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} | alternative_names= | pilgrimage_sites= | annual_festival= | rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} }} '''Kamoshimo Shrine''' (鴨下神社, かもしもじんじゃ) is a **massha** (末社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. The **three enshrined deities** are considered **water deities** of the local region.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 59Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 68. == Overview == Kamoshimo Shrine is located in {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is one of **13 massha** of the **Inner Shrine (Naikū) located in Tamaki Town** and holds the **highest Rank** among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 13–14 Approximately **2.5 km away** from Kamoshimo Shrine is {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|ja|鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}}, an **Inner Shrine sessha** (摂社), which enshrines the **same deities** and shares a connection with Kamoshimo Shrine, as both are located in **Shirota-no-go (城田郷)**.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p. 200. The name **"Kamo"** was traditionally believed to derive from an **old place name**. However, with the excavation of the **Saikū Site (斎宮遺跡)**, a connection to the **Kamo clan (賀茂氏)**, who served as **water administrators (水司)**, has been proposed.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p. 199. The name **"Shimo" (下, "lower")** refers to its position at the **foot of a mountain**, in contrast to **Kamo Shrine**, which is located **midway up the mountain**.. The shrine grounds cover **3 tan, 9 se, 14 bu (approximately 3,914 m²)**Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p. 13, surrounded by **grape vineyards**. The shrine hall is built in **Shinmei-zukuri (神明造)** style with **ita-buki (板葺, plank roofing)**, enclosed by a **single-layered tamagaki fence (玉垣)**. There is **no offertory box (賽銭箱)**. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: - **Ishi-Korowake-no-Mikoto (石己呂和居命)** - **Kamo-Hiko-no-Mikoto (鴨比古命)** - **Kamo-Hime-no-Mikoto (鴨比賣命)** All three deities are considered **guardian deities of water management and irrigation in Katsuta (勝田)**. - **Ishi-Korowake-no-Mikoto** is the **child of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命)**, the **same deity enshrined at Kamo Shrine**. - **Kamo-Hiko-no-Mikoto and Kamo-Hime-no-Mikoto** are **male and female deities** whose names derive from the **common noun "Kamo" (鴨, "duck")**, symbolizing their dual nature. == History == Kamoshimo Shrine is a **Shikigai-sha (式外社)**, meaning it is **not listed in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō (延喜式神名帳)**. However, since it is classified as an **Ise Grand Shrine massha**, it must have existed before the **compilation of the Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō (皇大神宮儀式帳)**, that is, before **Enryaku 23 (804)**.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22 Like Kamo Shrine, Kamoshimo Shrine was **originally regulated to follow the same 20-year rebuilding cycle** (*shikinen sengū*, 神宮式年遷宮) as the **Ise Grand Shrine**, overseen by the **Zōkyōshi (造宮使)**. During **ancient times**, Kamoshimo Shrine was categorized as a **"Taisha" (田社, "Rice Paddy Shrine")**. However, it was the **only Taisha shrine** to receive such **special treatment**. However, due to the **political turmoil of the Sengoku period (戦国時代)**, the shrine fell into neglect and was left in **a state of disrepair**.Kaneko (1983): p. 61. In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was **restored** along with {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}}.Sakurai (1991): pp. 280–281. == Access == === Public Transportation === - **From Tamaki Station (田丸駅) on the JR Sangu Line (参宮線)** ** Approximately **24 minutes on foot (2.0 km southwest)** === By Car === - **From Tamaki IC (玉城IC) on the Ise Expressway (伊勢自動車道)** ** Take **Mie Prefectural Route 65 (三重県道65号度会玉城線, Sunny Road)** north for approximately **3 minutes (1.3 km)** ** **No designated parking lot available** == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * Ise Bunka-sha, ed. *O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage* (お伊勢さん125社めぐり), Ise Bunka-sha, December 23, 2008, 151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4. * Ujiyamada City Hall, ed. *Ujiyamada City History, Vol. 1* (宇治山田市史 上巻), Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, 1929, 862p. * Gakken Publishing, *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine* (伊勢神宮に行こう), Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by Sonoda Minoru, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1. * Kaneko, Nobuo. *Tamaki Town History, Vol. 1: The History and Legends of Southern Ise* (玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―), Mie Prefecture Historical Materials Series No. 61, Mie Prefecture Historical Society, August 10, 1983, 276p. * Sakurai, Katsunoshin. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine* (伊勢神宮の祖型と展開), Kokusho Publishing, November 30, 1991, 318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3. * Shikinaisha Research Group, ed. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6: Tōkaidō 1* (式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1), Kōgakkan University Press, February 28, 1990, 690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8. {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Massha of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Kamoshimo Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ise Province]] [[Category:Massha and Sessha]] [[Category:Shikigai-sha]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:Buildings in Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:鴨下神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:式外社]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamoshimo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:鴨下神社]] [[sn:Kamoshimo Shrine]] [[sn:amoshimo Shrine]]

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] to [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Shrine | name= Kamoshimo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image=[[File:Kamoshimo-jinja (Naiku) 03.jpg|270px|Kamoshimo Shrine]] | location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町|12=draft}} Katsuta Aza Uenoyama 3642 | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 28 | lat_seconds = 44.8 | longitude = 136 | lon_minutes = 37 | lon_seconds = 16.3 | map= Japan Mie | enshrined_deities= Ishi-Korowake-no-Mikoto (石己呂和居命)
Kamo-Hiko-no-Mikoto (鴨比古命)
Kamo-Hime-no-Mikoto (鴨比賣命) | shrine_Rank= {{ill|Shikigai-sha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|12=draft}}
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} Massha | founded= Before Enryaku 23 (804) | main_hall= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} | alternative_names= | pilgrimage_sites= | annual_festival= | rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}} }} '''Kamoshimo Shrine''' (鴨下神社, かもしもじんじゃ) is a **massha** (末社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} (Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}. The **three enshrined deities** are considered **water deities** of the local region.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 59Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 68. == Overview == Kamoshimo Shrine is located in {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}. It is one of **13 massha** of the **Inner Shrine (Naikū) located in Tamaki Town** and holds the **highest Rank** among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 13–14 Approximately **2.5 km away** from Kamoshimo Shrine is {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|ja|鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|12=draft}}, an **Inner Shrine sessha** (摂社), which enshrines the **same deities** and shares a connection with Kamoshimo Shrine, as both are located in **Shirota-no-go (城田郷)**.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p. 200. The name **"Kamo"** was traditionally believed to derive from an **old place name**. However, with the excavation of the **Saikū Site (斎宮遺跡)**, a connection to the **Kamo clan (賀茂氏)**, who served as **water administrators (水司)**, has been proposed.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p. 199. The name **"Shimo" (下, "lower")** refers to its position at the **foot of a mountain**, in contrast to **Kamo Shrine**, which is located **midway up the mountain**.. The shrine grounds cover **3 tan, 9 se, 14 bu (approximately 3,914 m²)**Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p. 13, surrounded by **grape vineyards**. The shrine hall is built in **Shinmei-zukuri (神明造)** style with **ita-buki (板葺, plank roofing)**, enclosed by a **single-layered tamagaki fence (玉垣)**. There is **no offertory box (賽銭箱)**. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: - **Ishi-Korowake-no-Mikoto (石己呂和居命)** - **Kamo-Hiko-no-Mikoto (鴨比古命)** - **Kamo-Hime-no-Mikoto (鴨比賣命)** All three deities are considered **guardian deities of water management and irrigation in Katsuta (勝田)**. - **Ishi-Korowake-no-Mikoto** is the **child of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命)**, the **same deity enshrined at Kamo Shrine**. - **Kamo-Hiko-no-Mikoto and Kamo-Hime-no-Mikoto** are **male and female deities** whose names derive from the **common noun "Kamo" (鴨, "duck")**, symbolizing their dual nature. == History == Kamoshimo Shrine is a **Shikigai-sha (式外社)**, meaning it is **not listed in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō (延喜式神名帳)**. However, since it is classified as an **Ise Grand Shrine massha**, it must have existed before the **compilation of the Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō (皇大神宮儀式帳)**, that is, before **Enryaku 23 (804)**.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22 Like Kamo Shrine, Kamoshimo Shrine was **originally regulated to follow the same 20-year rebuilding cycle** (*shikinen sengū*, 神宮式年遷宮) as the **Ise Grand Shrine**, overseen by the **Zōkyōshi (造宮使)**. During **ancient times**, Kamoshimo Shrine was categorized as a **"Taisha" (田社, "Rice Paddy Shrine")**. However, it was the **only Taisha shrine** to receive such **special treatment**. However, due to the **political turmoil of the Sengoku period (戦国時代)**, the shrine fell into neglect and was left in **a state of disrepair**.Kaneko (1983): p. 61. In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was **restored** along with {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|12=draft}}.Sakurai (1991): pp. 280–281. == Access == === Public Transportation === - **From Tamaki Station (田丸駅) on the JR Sangu Line (参宮線)** ** Approximately **24 minutes on foot (2.0 km southwest)** === By Car === - **From Tamaki IC (玉城IC) on the Ise Expressway (伊勢自動車道)** ** Take **Mie Prefectural Route 65 (三重県道65号度会玉城線, Sunny Road)** north for approximately **3 minutes (1.3 km)** ** **No designated parking lot available** == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * Ise Bunka-sha, ed. *O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage* (お伊勢さん125社めぐり), Ise Bunka-sha, December 23, 2008, 151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4. * Ujiyamada City Hall, ed. *Ujiyamada City History, Vol. 1* (宇治山田市史 上巻), Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, 1929, 862p. * Gakken Publishing, *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine* (伊勢神宮に行こう), Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by Sonoda Minoru, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1. * Kaneko, Nobuo. *Tamaki Town History, Vol. 1: The History and Legends of Southern Ise* (玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―), Mie Prefecture Historical Materials Series No. 61, Mie Prefecture Historical Society, August 10, 1983, 276p. * Sakurai, Katsunoshin. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine* (伊勢神宮の祖型と展開), Kokusho Publishing, November 30, 1991, 318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3. * Shikinaisha Research Group, ed. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6: Tōkaidō 1* (式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1), Kōgakkan University Press, February 28, 1990, 690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8. {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Massha of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Kamoshimo Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ise Province]] [[Category:Massha and Sessha]] [[Category:Shikigai-sha]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:Buildings in Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:鴨下神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:式外社]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamoshimo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:鴨下神社]] [[sn:Kamoshimo Shrine]] [[sn:amoshimo Shrine]]

{{Shrine | name= Kamoshimo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image=[[File:Kamoshimo-jinja (Naiku) 03.jpg|270px|Kamoshimo Shrine]] | location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡}}, {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町}} Katsuta Aza Uenoyama 3642 | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 28 | lat_seconds = 44.8 | longitude = 136 | lon_minutes = 37 | lon_seconds = 16.3 | map= Japan Mie | enshrined_deities= Ishi-Korowake-no-Mikoto (石己呂和居命)
Kamo-Hiko-no-Mikoto (鴨比古命)
Kamo-Hime-no-Mikoto (鴨比賣命) | shrine_Rank= {{ill|Shikigai-sha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha}}
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} Massha | founded= Before Enryaku 23 (804) | main_hall= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} | alternative_names= | pilgrimage_sites= | annual_festival= | rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} }} '''Kamoshimo Shrine''' (鴨下神社, かもしもじんじゃ) is a **massha** (末社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. The **three enshrined deities** are considered **water deities** of the local region.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 59Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 68. == Overview == Kamoshimo Shrine is located in {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is one of **13 massha** of the **Inner Shrine (Naikū) located in Tamaki Town** and holds the **highest Rank** among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 13–14 Approximately **2.5 km away** from Kamoshimo Shrine is {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|ja|鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo Shrine}}, an **Inner Shrine sessha** (摂社), which enshrines the **same deities** and shares a connection with Kamoshimo Shrine, as both are located in **Shirota-no-go (城田郷)**.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p. 200. The name **"Kamo"** was traditionally believed to derive from an **old place name**. However, with the excavation of the **Saikū Site (斎宮遺跡)**, a connection to the **Kamo clan (賀茂氏)**, who served as **water administrators (水司)**, has been proposed.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p. 199. The name **"Shimo" (下, "lower")** refers to its position at the **foot of a mountain**, in contrast to **Kamo Shrine**, which is located **midway up the mountain**.. The shrine grounds cover **3 tan, 9 se, 14 bu (approximately 3,914 m²)**Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p. 13, surrounded by **grape vineyards**. The shrine hall is built in **Shinmei-zukuri (神明造)** style with **ita-buki (板葺, plank roofing)**, enclosed by a **single-layered tamagaki fence (玉垣)**. There is **no offertory box (賽銭箱)**. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: - **Ishi-Korowake-no-Mikoto (石己呂和居命)** - **Kamo-Hiko-no-Mikoto (鴨比古命)** - **Kamo-Hime-no-Mikoto (鴨比賣命)** All three deities are considered **guardian deities of water management and irrigation in Katsuta (勝田)**. - **Ishi-Korowake-no-Mikoto** is the **child of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命)**, the **same deity enshrined at Kamo Shrine**. - **Kamo-Hiko-no-Mikoto and Kamo-Hime-no-Mikoto** are **male and female deities** whose names derive from the **common noun "Kamo" (鴨, "duck")**, symbolizing their dual nature. == History == Kamoshimo Shrine is a **Shikigai-sha (式外社)**, meaning it is **not listed in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō (延喜式神名帳)**. However, since it is classified as an **Ise Grand Shrine massha**, it must have existed before the **compilation of the Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō (皇大神宮儀式帳)**, that is, before **Enryaku 23 (804)**.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22 Like Kamo Shrine, Kamoshimo Shrine was **originally regulated to follow the same 20-year rebuilding cycle** (*shikinen sengū*, 神宮式年遷宮) as the **Ise Grand Shrine**, overseen by the **Zōkyōshi (造宮使)**. During **ancient times**, Kamoshimo Shrine was categorized as a **"Taisha" (田社, "Rice Paddy Shrine")**. However, it was the **only Taisha shrine** to receive such **special treatment**. However, due to the **political turmoil of the Sengoku period (戦国時代)**, the shrine fell into neglect and was left in **a state of disrepair**.Kaneko (1983): p. 61. In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was **restored** along with {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine}}.Sakurai (1991): pp. 280–281. == Access == === Public Transportation === - **From Tamaki Station (田丸駅) on the JR Sangu Line (参宮線)** ** Approximately **24 minutes on foot (2.0 km southwest)** === By Car === - **From Tamaki IC (玉城IC) on the Ise Expressway (伊勢自動車道)** ** Take **Mie Prefectural Route 65 (三重県道65号度会玉城線, Sunny Road)** north for approximately **3 minutes (1.3 km)** ** **No designated parking lot available** == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * Ise Bunka-sha, ed. *O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage* (お伊勢さん125社めぐり), Ise Bunka-sha, December 23, 2008, 151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4. * Ujiyamada City Hall, ed. *Ujiyamada City History, Vol. 1* (宇治山田市史 上巻), Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, 1929, 862p. * Gakken Publishing, *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine* (伊勢神宮に行こう), Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by Sonoda Minoru, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1. * Kaneko, Nobuo. *Tamaki Town History, Vol. 1: The History and Legends of Southern Ise* (玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―), Mie Prefecture Historical Materials Series No. 61, Mie Prefecture Historical Society, August 10, 1983, 276p. * Sakurai, Katsunoshin. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine* (伊勢神宮の祖型と展開), Kokusho Publishing, November 30, 1991, 318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3. * Shikinaisha Research Group, ed. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6: Tōkaidō 1* (式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1), Kōgakkan University Press, February 28, 1990, 690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8. {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Massha of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Kamoshimo Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ise Province]] [[Category:Massha and Sessha]] [[Category:Shikigai-sha]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:Buildings in Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:鴨下神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:式外社]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kamoshimo Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:鴨下神社]] [[sn:Kamoshimo Shrine]] [[sn:amoshimo Shrine]]

{{Shrine | name= Kamoshimo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image=[[File:Kamoshimo-jinja (Naiku) 03.jpg|270px|Kamoshimo Shrine]] | location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町|12=draft|12=draft}} Katsuta Aza Uenoyama 3642 | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 28 | lat_seconds = 44.8 | longitude = 136 | lon_minutes = 37 | lon_seconds = 16.3 | map= Japan Mie | enshrined_deities= Ishi-Korowake-no-Mikoto (石己呂和居命)
Kamo-Hiko-no-Mikoto (鴨比古命)
Kamo-Hime-no-Mikoto (鴨比賣命) | shrine_Rank= {{ill|Shikigai-sha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} Massha | founded= Before Enryaku 23 (804) | main_hall= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} | alternative_names= | pilgrimage_sites= | annual_festival= | rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} }} '''Kamoshimo Shrine''' (鴨下神社, かもしもじんじゃ) is a **massha** (末社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. The **three enshrined deities** are considered **water deities** of the local region.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 59Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 68. == Overview == Kamoshimo Shrine is located in {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is one of **13 massha** of the **Inner Shrine (Naikū) located in Tamaki Town** and holds the **highest Rank** among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 13–14 Approximately **2.5 km away** from Kamoshimo Shrine is {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|ja|鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}}, an **Inner Shrine sessha** (摂社), which enshrines the **same deities** and shares a connection with Kamoshimo Shrine, as both are located in **Shirota-no-go (城田郷)**.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p. 200. The name **"Kamo"** was traditionally believed to derive from an **old place name**. However, with the excavation of the **Saikū Site (斎宮遺跡)**, a connection to the **Kamo clan (賀茂氏)**, who served as **water administrators (水司)**, has been proposed.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p. 199. The name **"Shimo" (下, "lower")** refers to its position at the **foot of a mountain**, in contrast to **Kamo Shrine**, which is located **midway up the mountain**.. The shrine grounds cover **3 tan, 9 se, 14 bu (approximately 3,914 m²)**Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p. 13, surrounded by **grape vineyards**. The shrine hall is built in **Shinmei-zukuri (神明造)** style with **ita-buki (板葺, plank roofing)**, enclosed by a **single-layered tamagaki fence (玉垣)**. There is **no offertory box (賽銭箱)**. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: - **Ishi-Korowake-no-Mikoto (石己呂和居命)** - **Kamo-Hiko-no-Mikoto (鴨比古命)** - **Kamo-Hime-no-Mikoto (鴨比賣命)** All three deities are considered **guardian deities of water management and irrigation in Katsuta (勝田)**. - **Ishi-Korowake-no-Mikoto** is the **child of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命)**, the **same deity enshrined at Kamo Shrine**. - **Kamo-Hiko-no-Mikoto and Kamo-Hime-no-Mikoto** are **male and female deities** whose names derive from the **common noun "Kamo" (鴨, "duck")**, symbolizing their dual nature. == History == Kamoshimo Shrine is a **Shikigai-sha (式外社)**, meaning it is **not listed in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō (延喜式神名帳)**. However, since it is classified as an **Ise Grand Shrine massha**, it must have existed before the **compilation of the Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō (皇大神宮儀式帳)**, that is, before **Enryaku 23 (804)**.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22 Like Kamo Shrine, Kamoshimo Shrine was **originally regulated to follow the same 20-year rebuilding cycle** (*shikinen sengū*, 神宮式年遷宮) as the **Ise Grand Shrine**, overseen by the **Zōkyōshi (造宮使)**. During **ancient times**, Kamoshimo Shrine was categorized as a **"Taisha" (田社, "Rice Paddy Shrine")**. However, it was the **only Taisha shrine** to receive such **special treatment**. However, due to the **political turmoil of the Sengoku period (戦国時代)**, the shrine fell into neglect and was left in **a state of disrepair**.Kaneko (1983): p. 61. In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was **restored** along with {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}}.Sakurai (1991): pp. 280–281. == Access == === Public Transportation === - **From Tamaki Station (田丸駅) on the JR Sangu Line (参宮線)** ** Approximately **24 minutes on foot (2.0 km southwest)** === By Car === - **From Tamaki IC (玉城IC) on the Ise Expressway (伊勢自動車道)** ** Take **Mie Prefectural Route 65 (三重県道65号度会玉城線, Sunny Road)** north for approximately **3 minutes (1.3 km)** ** **No designated parking lot available** == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * Ise Bunka-sha, ed. *O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage* (お伊勢さん125社めぐり), Ise Bunka-sha, December 23, 2008, 151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4. * Ujiyamada City Hall, ed. *Ujiyamada City History, Vol. 1* (宇治山田市史 上巻), Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, 1929, 862p. * Gakken Publishing, *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine* (伊勢神宮に行こう), Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by Sonoda Minoru, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1. * Kaneko, Nobuo. *Tamaki Town History, Vol. 1: The History and Legends of Southern Ise* (玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―), Mie Prefecture Historical Materials Series No. 61, Mie Prefecture Historical Society, August 10, 1983, 276p. * Sakurai, Katsunoshin. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine* (伊勢神宮の祖型と展開), Kokusho Publishing, November 30, 1991, 318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3. * Shikinaisha Research Group, ed. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6: Tōkaidō 1* (式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1), Kōgakkan University Press, February 28, 1990, 690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8. {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|鴨下神社|version=76118870|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Massha of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Kamoshimo Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Ise Province]] [[Category:Massha and Sessha]] [[Category:Shikigai-sha]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:Buildings in Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:鴨下神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:式外社]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:category refresh]] }} [[sn:Kamoshimo Shrine]] [[ja:鴨下神社]] [[sn:amoshimo Shrine]]

ISBN 4-336-03296-3. * Shikinaisha Research Group, ed. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6: Tōkaidō 1* (式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1), Kōgakkan University Press, February 28, 1990, 690p. {{Shrine | name= Kamoshimo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image=[[File:Kamoshimo-jinja (Naiku) 03.jpg|270px|Kamoshimo Shrine]] | location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Katsuta Aza Uenoyama 3642 | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 28 | lat_seconds = 44.8 | longitude = 136 | lon_minutes = 37 | lon_seconds = 16.3 | map= Japan Mie | enshrined_deities= Ishi-Korowake-no-Mikoto (石己呂和居命)
Kamo-Hiko-no-Mikoto (鴨比古命)
Kamo-Hime-no-Mikoto (鴨比賣命) | shrine_Rank= {{ill|Shikigai-sha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Massha | founded= Before Enryaku 23 (804) | main_hall= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | alternative_names= | pilgrimage_sites= | annual_festival= | rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} }} '''Kamoshimo Shrine''' (鴨下神社, かもしもじんじゃ) is a **massha** (末社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The **three enshrined deities** are considered **water deities** of the local region.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 59Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 68. == Overview == Kamoshimo Shrine is located in {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is one of **13 massha** of the **Inner Shrine (Naikū) located in Tamaki Town** and holds the **highest Rank** among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 13–14 Approximately **2.5 km away** from Kamoshimo Shrine is {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|ja|鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, an **Inner Shrine sessha** (摂社), which enshrines the **same deities** and shares a connection with Kamoshimo Shrine, as both are located in **Shirota-no-go (城田郷)**.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p. 200. The name **"Kamo"** was traditionally believed to derive from an **old place name**. However, with the excavation of the **Saikū Site (斎宮遺跡)**, a connection to the **Kamo clan (賀茂氏)**, who served as **water administrators (水司)**, has been proposed.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p. 199. The name **"Shimo" (下, "lower")** refers to its position at the **foot of a mountain**, in contrast to **Kamo Shrine**, which is located **midway up the mountain**.. The shrine grounds cover **3 tan, 9 se, 14 bu (approximately 3,914 m²)**Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p. 13, surrounded by **grape vineyards**. The shrine hall is built in **Shinmei-zukuri (神明造)** style with **ita-buki (板葺, plank roofing)**, enclosed by a **single-layered tamagaki fence (玉垣)**. There is **no offertory box (賽銭箱)**. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: - **Ishi-Korowake-no-Mikoto (石己呂和居命)** - **Kamo-Hiko-no-Mikoto (鴨比古命)** - **Kamo-Hime-no-Mikoto (鴨比賣命)** All three deities are considered **guardian deities of water management and irrigation in Katsuta (勝田)**. - **Ishi-Korowake-no-Mikoto** is the **child of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命)**, the **same deity enshrined at Kamo Shrine**. - **Kamo-Hiko-no-Mikoto and Kamo-Hime-no-Mikoto** are **male and female deities** whose names derive from the **common noun "Kamo" (鴨, "duck")**, symbolizing their dual nature. == History == Kamoshimo Shrine is a **Shikigai-sha (式外社)**, meaning it is **not listed in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō (延喜式神名帳)**. However, since it is classified as an **Ise Grand Shrine massha**, it must have existed before the **compilation of the Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō (皇大神宮儀式帳)**, that is, before **Enryaku 23 (804)**.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22 Like Kamo Shrine, Kamoshimo Shrine was **originally regulated to follow the same 20-year rebuilding cycle** (*shikinen sengū*, 神宮式年遷宮) as the **Ise Grand Shrine**, overseen by the **Zōkyōshi (造宮使)**. During **ancient times**, Kamoshimo Shrine was categorized as a **"Taisha" (田社, "Rice Paddy Shrine")**. However, it was the **only Taisha shrine** to receive such **special treatment**. However, due to the **political turmoil of the Sengoku period (戦国時代)**, the shrine fell into neglect and was left in **a state of disrepair**.Kaneko (1983): p. 61. In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was **restored** along with {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Sakurai (1991): pp. 280–281. == Access == === Public Transportation === - **From Tamaki Station (田丸駅) on the JR Sangu Line (参宮線)** ** Approximately **24 minutes on foot (2.0 km southwest)** === By Car === - **From Tamaki IC (玉城IC) on the Ise Expressway (伊勢自動車道)** ** Take **Mie Prefectural Route 65 (三重県道65号度会玉城線, Sunny Road)** north for approximately **3 minutes (1.3 km)** ** **No designated parking lot available** == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * Ise Bunka-sha, ed. *O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage* (お伊勢さん125社めぐり), Ise Bunka-sha, December 23, 2008, 151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4. * Ujiyamada City Hall, ed. *Ujiyamada City History, Vol. 1* (宇治山田市史 上巻), Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, 1929, 862p. * Gakken Publishing, *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine* (伊勢神宮に行こう), Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by Sonoda Minoru, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1. * Kaneko, Nobuo. *Tamaki Town History, Vol. 1: The History and Legends of Southern Ise* (玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―), Mie Prefecture Historical Materials Series No. 61, Mie Prefecture Historical Society, August 10, 1983, 276p. * Sakurai, Katsunoshin. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine* (伊勢神宮の祖型と展開), Kokusho Publishing, November 30, 1991, 318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3. * Shikinaisha Research Group, ed. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6: Tōkaidō 1* (式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1), Kōgakkan University Press, February 28, 1990, 690p. However, due to the **political turmoil of the Sengoku period (戦国時代)**, the shrine fell into neglect and was left in **a state of disrepair**.Kaneko (1983): p. 61. In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was **restored** along with {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Sakurai (1991): pp. 280–281. == Access == === Public Transportation === - **From Tamaki Station (田丸駅) on the JR Sangu Line (参宮線)** ** Approximately **24 minutes on foot (2.0 km southwest)** === By Car === - **From Tamaki IC (玉城IC) on the Ise Expressway (伊勢自動車道)** ** Take **Mie Prefectural Route 65 (三重県道65号度会玉城線, Sunny Road)** north for approximately **3 minutes (1.3 km)** ** **No designated parking lot available** == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * Ise Bunka-sha, ed. *O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage* (お伊勢さん125社めぐり), Ise Bunka-sha, December 23, 2008, 151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4. * Ujiyamada City Hall, ed. *Ujiyamada City History, Vol. 1* (宇治山田市史 上巻), Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, 1929, 862p. * Gakken Publishing, *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine* (伊勢神宮に行こう), Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by Sonoda Minoru, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1. * Kaneko, Nobuo. *Tamaki Town History, Vol. 1: The History and Legends of Southern Ise* (玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―), Mie Prefecture Historical Materials Series No. 61, Mie Prefecture Historical Society, August 10, 1983, 276p. * Sakurai, Katsunoshin. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine* (伊勢神宮の祖型と展開), Kokusho Publishing, November 30, 1991, 318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3. * Shikinaisha Research Group, ed. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6: Tōkaidō 1* (式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1), Kōgakkan University Press, February 28, 1990, 690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8. {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|鴨下神社|version=76118870|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Massha of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Kamoshimo Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Massha and Sessha]] [[Category:Shikigai-sha]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:Buildings in Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:鴨下神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikigeisha]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] }} [[sn:Kamoshimo Shrine]] [[ja:鴨下神社]] [[sn:amoshimo Shrine]]

{{Shrine | name= Kamoshimo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image=[[File:Kamoshimo-jinja (Naiku) 03.jpg|270px|Kamoshimo Shrine]] | location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Katsuta Aza Uenoyama 3642 | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 28 | lat_seconds = 44.8 | longitude = 136 | lon_minutes = 37 | lon_seconds = 16.3 | map= Japan Mie | enshrined_deities= Ishi-Korowake-no-Mikoto (石己呂和居命)
Kamo-Hiko-no-Mikoto (鴨比古命)
Kamo-Hime-no-Mikoto (鴨比賣命) | shrine_Rank= {{ill|Shikigai-sha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Massha | founded= Before Enryaku 23 (804) | main_hall= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | alternative_names= | pilgrimage_sites= | annual_festival= | rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} }} '''Kamoshimo Shrine''' (鴨下神社, かもしもじんじゃ) is a **massha** (末社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The **three enshrined deities** are considered **water deities** of the local region.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 59Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 68. == Overview == Kamoshimo Shrine is located in {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is one of **13 massha** of the **Inner Shrine (Naikū) located in Tamaki Town** and holds the **highest Rank** among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 13–14 Approximately **2.5 km away** from Kamoshimo Shrine is {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|ja|鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, an **Inner Shrine sessha** (摂社), which enshrines the **same deities** and shares a connection with Kamoshimo Shrine, as both are located in **Shirota-no-go (城田郷)**.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p. 200. The name **"Kamo"** was traditionally believed to derive from an **old place name**. However, with the excavation of the **Saikū Site (斎宮遺跡)**, a connection to the **Kamo clan (賀茂氏)**, who served as **water administrators (水司)**, has been proposed.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p. 199. The name **"Shimo" (下, "lower")** refers to its position at the **foot of a mountain**, in contrast to **Kamo Shrine**, which is located **midway up the mountain**.. The shrine grounds cover **3 tan, 9 se, 14 bu (approximately 3,914 m²)**Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p. 13, surrounded by **grape vineyards**. The shrine hall is built in **Shinmei-zukuri (神明造)** style with **ita-buki (板葺, plank roofing)**, enclosed by a **single-layered tamagaki fence (玉垣)**. There is **no offertory box (賽銭箱)**. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: - **Ishi-Korowake-no-Mikoto (石己呂和居命)** - **Kamo-Hiko-no-Mikoto (鴨比古命)** - **Kamo-Hime-no-Mikoto (鴨比賣命)** All three deities are considered **guardian deities of water management and irrigation in Katsuta (勝田)**. - **Ishi-Korowake-no-Mikoto** is the **child of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命)**, the **same deity enshrined at Kamo Shrine**. - **Kamo-Hiko-no-Mikoto and Kamo-Hime-no-Mikoto** are **male and female deities** whose names derive from the **common noun "Kamo" (鴨, "duck")**, symbolizing their dual nature. == History == Kamoshimo Shrine is a **Shikigai-sha (式外社)**, meaning it is **not listed in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō (延喜式神名帳)**. However, since it is classified as an **Ise Grand Shrine massha**, it must have existed before the **compilation of the Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō (皇大神宮儀式帳)**, that is, before **Enryaku 23 (804)**.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22 Like Kamo Shrine, Kamoshimo Shrine was **originally regulated to follow the same 20-year rebuilding cycle** (*shikinen sengū*, 神宮式年遷宮) as the **Ise Grand Shrine**, overseen by the **Zōkyōshi (造宮使)**. During **ancient times**, Kamoshimo Shrine was categorized as a **"Taisha" (田社, "Rice Paddy Shrine")**. However, it was the **only Taisha shrine** to receive such **special treatment**. However, due to the **political turmoil of the Sengoku period (戦国時代)**, the shrine fell into neglect and was left in **a state of disrepair**.Kaneko (1983): p. 61. In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was **restored** along with {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Sakurai (1991): pp. 280–281. == Access == === Public Transportation === - **From Tamaki Station (田丸駅) on the JR Sangu Line (参宮線)** ** Approximately **24 minutes on foot (2.0 km southwest)** === By Car === - **From Tamaki IC (玉城IC) on the Ise Expressway (伊勢自動車道)** ** Take **Mie Prefectural Route 65 (三重県道65号度会玉城線, Sunny Road)** north for approximately **3 minutes (1.3 km)** ** **No designated parking lot available** == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * Ise Bunka-sha, ed. *O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage* (お伊勢さん125社めぐり), Ise Bunka-sha, December 23, 2008, 151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4. * Ujiyamada City Hall, ed. *Ujiyamada City History, Vol. 1* (宇治山田市史 上巻), Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, 1929, 862p. * Gakken Publishing, *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine* (伊勢神宮に行こう), Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by Sonoda Minoru, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1. * Kaneko, Nobuo. *Tamaki Town History, Vol. 1: The History and Legends of Southern Ise* (玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―), Mie Prefecture Historical Materials Series No. 61, Mie Prefecture Historical Society, August 10, 1983, 276p. * Sakurai, Katsunoshin. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine* (伊勢神宮の祖型と展開), Kokusho Publishing, November 30, 1991, 318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3. * Shikinaisha Research Group, ed. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6: Tōkaidō 1* (式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1), Kōgakkan University Press, February 28, 1990, 690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8. {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|鴨下神社|version=76118870|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Massha of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Kamoshimo Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Massha and Sessha]] [[Category:Shikigai-sha]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:Buildings in Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:鴨下神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikigeisha]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] }} [[sn:Kamoshimo Shrine]] [[ja:鴨下神社]] [[sn:amoshimo Shrine]]

There is **no offertory box (賽銭箱)**. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: - **Ishi-Korowake-no-Mikoto (石己呂和居命)** - **Kamo-Hiko-no-Mikoto (鴨比古命)** - **Kamo-Hime-no-Mikoto (鴨比賣命)** All three deities are considered **guardian deities of water management and irrigation in Katsuta (勝田)**. - **Ishi-Korowake-no-Mikoto** is the **child of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命)**, the **same deity enshrined at Kamo Shrine**. - **Kamo-Hiko-no-Mikoto and Kamo-Hime-no-Mikoto** are **male and female deities** whose names derive from the **common noun "Kamo" (鴨, "duck")**, symbolizing their dual nature. == History == Kamoshimo Shrine is a **Shikigai-sha (式外社)**, meaning it is **not listed in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō (延喜式神名帳)**. However, since it is classified as an **Ise Grand Shrine massha**, it must have existed before the **compilation of the Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō (皇大神宮儀式帳)**, that is, before **Enryaku 23 (804)**.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22 Like Kamo Shrine, Kamoshimo Shrine was **originally regulated to follow the same 20-year rebuilding cycle** (*shikinen sengū*, 神宮式年遷宮) as the **Ise Grand Shrine**, overseen by the **Zōkyōshi (造宮使)**. During **ancient times**, Kamoshimo Shrine was categorized as a **"Taisha" (田社, "Rice Paddy Shrine")**. However, it was the **only Taisha shrine** to receive such **special treatment**. However, due to the **political turmoil of the Sengoku period (戦国時代)**, the shrine fell into neglect and was left in **a state of disrepair**.Kaneko (1983): p. 61. In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was **restored** along with {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Sakurai (1991): pp. 280–281. == Access == === Public Transportation === - **From Tamaki Station (田丸駅) on the JR Sangu Line (参宮線)** ** Approximately **24 minutes on foot (2.0 km southwest)** === By Car === - **From Tamaki IC (玉城IC) on the Ise Expressway (伊勢自動車道)** ** Take **Mie Prefectural Route 65 (三重県道65号度会玉城線, Sunny Road)** north for approximately **3 minutes (1.3 km)** ** **No designated parking lot available** == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * Ise Bunka-sha, ed. *O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage* (お伊勢さん125社めぐり), Ise Bunka-sha, December 23, 2008, 151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4. * Ujiyamada City Hall, ed. *Ujiyamada City History, Vol. 1* (宇治山田市史 上巻), Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, 1929, 862p. * Gakken Publishing, *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine* (伊勢神宮に行こう), Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by Sonoda Minoru, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1. * Kaneko, Nobuo. *Tamaki Town History, Vol. 1: The History and Legends of Southern Ise* (玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―), Mie Prefecture Historical Materials Series No. 61, Mie Prefecture Historical Society, August 10, 1983, 276p. * Sakurai, Katsunoshin. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine* (伊勢神宮の祖型と展開), Kokusho Publishing, November 30, 1991, 318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3. * Shikinaisha Research Group, ed. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6: Tōkaidō 1* (式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1), Kōgakkan University Press, February 28, 1990, 690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8. {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|鴨下神社|version=76118870|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Massha of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Kamoshimo Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Massha and Sessha]] [[Category:Shikigai-sha]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:Buildings in Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:鴨下神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikigeisha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[sn:Kamoshimo Shrine]] [[ja:鴨下神社]] [[sn:amoshimo Shrine]]

{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} Massha | founded= Before Enryaku 23 (804) | main_hall= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} | alternative_names= | pilgrimage_sites= | annual_festival= | rituals= {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} }} '''Kamoshimo Shrine''' (鴨下神社, かもしもじんじゃ) is a **massha** (末社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}. The **three enshrined deities** are considered **water deities** of the local region.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 59Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 68. == Overview == Kamoshimo Shrine is located in {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki, Mie|qq=}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai District, Mie|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}. It is one of **13 massha** of the **Inner Shrine (Naikū) located in Tamaki Town** and holds the **highest Rank** among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 13–14 Approximately **2.5 km away** from Kamoshimo Shrine is {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|ja|鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, an **Inner Shrine sessha** (摂社), which enshrines the **same deities** and shares a connection with Kamoshimo Shrine, as both are located in **Shirota-no-go (城田郷)**.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p. 200. The name **"Kamo"** was traditionally believed to derive from an **old place name**. However, with the excavation of the **Saikū Site (斎宮遺跡)**, a connection to the **Kamo clan (賀茂氏)**, who served as **water administrators (水司)**, has been proposed.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p. 199. The name **"Shimo" (下, "lower")** refers to its position at the **foot of a mountain**, in contrast to **Kamo Shrine**, which is located **midway up the mountain**.. The shrine grounds cover **3 tan, 9 se, 14 bu (approximately 3,914 m²)**Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p. 13, surrounded by **grape vineyards**. The shrine hall is built in **Shinmei-zukuri (神明造)** style with **ita-buki (板葺, plank roofing)**, enclosed by a **single-layered tamagaki fence (玉垣)**. There is **no offertory box (賽銭箱)**. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: - **Ishi-Korowake-no-Mikoto (石己呂和居命)** - **Kamo-Hiko-no-Mikoto (鴨比古命)** - **Kamo-Hime-no-Mikoto (鴨比賣命)** All three deities are considered **guardian deities of water management and irrigation in Katsuta (勝田)**. - **Ishi-Korowake-no-Mikoto** is the **child of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命)**, the **same deity enshrined at Kamo Shrine**. - **Kamo-Hiko-no-Mikoto and Kamo-Hime-no-Mikoto** are **male and female deities** whose names derive from the **common noun "Kamo" (鴨, "duck")**, symbolizing their dual nature. == History == Kamoshimo Shrine is a **Shikigai-sha (式外社)**, meaning it is **not listed in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō (延喜式神名帳)**. However, since it is classified as an **Ise Grand Shrine massha**, it must have existed before the **compilation of the Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō (皇大神宮儀式帳)**, that is, before **Enryaku 23 (804)**.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22 Like Kamo Shrine, Kamoshimo Shrine was **originally regulated to follow the same 20-year rebuilding cycle** (*shikinen sengū*, 神宮式年遷宮) as the **Ise Grand Shrine**, overseen by the **Zōkyōshi (造宮使)**. During **ancient times**, Kamoshimo Shrine was categorized as a **"Taisha" (田社, "Rice Paddy Shrine")**. However, it was the **only Taisha shrine** to receive such **special treatment**. However, due to the **political turmoil of the Sengoku period (戦国時代)**, the shrine fell into neglect and was left in **a state of disrepair**.Kaneko (1983): p. 61. In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was **restored** along with {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyoda Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}.Sakurai (1991): pp. 280–281. == Access == === Public Transportation === - **From Tamaki Station (田丸駅) on the JR Sangu Line (参宮線)** ** Approximately **24 minutes on foot (2.0 km southwest)** === By Car === - **From Tamaki IC (玉城IC) on the Ise Expressway (伊勢自動車道)** ** Take **Mie Prefectural Route 65 (三重県道65号度会玉城線, Sunny Road)** north for approximately **3 minutes (1.3 km)** ** **No designated parking lot available** == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * Ise Bunka-sha, ed. *O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage* (お伊勢さん125社めぐり), Ise Bunka-sha, December 23, 2008, 151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4. * Ujiyamada City Hall, ed. *Ujiyamada City History, Vol. 1* (宇治山田市史 上巻), Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, 1929, 862p. * Gakken Publishing, *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine* (伊勢神宮に行こう), Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by Sonoda Minoru, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1. * Kaneko, Nobuo. *Tamaki Town History, Vol. 1: The History and Legends of Southern Ise* (玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―), Mie Prefecture Historical Materials Series No. 61, Mie Prefecture Historical Society, August 10, 1983, 276p. * Sakurai, Katsunoshin. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine* (伊勢神宮の祖型と展開), Kokusho Publishing, November 30, 1991, 318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3. * Shikinaisha Research Group, ed. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6: Tōkaidō 1* (式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1), Kōgakkan University Press, February 28, 1990, 690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8. {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|鴨下神社|version=76118870|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Massha of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Kamoshimo Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shikigai-sha]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:Buildings in Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikigeisha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[sn:Kamoshimo Shrine]] [[ja:鴨下神社]] [[sn:amoshimo Shrine]]

In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was **restored** along with {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyoda Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}.Sakurai (1991): pp. 280–281. == Access == === Public Transportation === - **From Tamaki Station (田丸駅) on the JR Sangu Line (参宮線)** ** Approximately **24 minutes on foot (2.0 km southwest)** === By Car === - **From Tamaki IC (玉城IC) on the Ise Expressway (伊勢自動車道)** ** Take **Mie Prefectural Route 65 (三重県道65号度会玉城線, Sunny Road)** north for approximately **3 minutes (1.3 km)** ** **No designated parking lot available** == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * Ise Bunka-sha, ed. *O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage* (お伊勢さん125社めぐり), Ise Bunka-sha, December 23, 2008, 151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4. * Ujiyamada City Hall, ed. *Ujiyamada City History, Vol. 1* (宇治山田市史 上巻), Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, 1929, 862p. * Gakken Publishing, *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine* (伊勢神宮に行こう), Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by Sonoda Minoru, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1. * Kaneko, Nobuo. *Tamaki Town History, Vol. 1: The History and Legends of Southern Ise* (玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―), Mie Prefecture Historical Materials Series No. 61, Mie Prefecture Historical Society, August 10, 1983, 276p. * Sakurai, Katsunoshin. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine* (伊勢神宮の祖型と展開), Kokusho Publishing, November 30, 1991, 318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3. * Shikinaisha Research Group, ed. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6: Tōkaidō 1* (式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1), Kōgakkan University Press, February 28, 1990, 690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8. {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|鴨下神社|version=76118870|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Massha of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Kamoshimo Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:Buildings in Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikigeisha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[sn:Kamoshimo Shrine]] [[ja:鴨下神社]] [[sn:amoshimo Shrine]]

revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
{{Shrine | name= Kamoshimo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image=[[File:Kamoshimo-jinja (Naiku) 03.jpg|270px|Kamoshimo Shrine]] | location = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District, Mie]], [[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki, Mie]] Katsuta Aza Uenoyama 3642 | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 28 | lat_seconds = 44.8 | longitude = 136 | lon_minutes = 37 | lon_seconds = 16.3 | map= Japan Mie | enshrined_deities= Ishi-Korowake-no-Mikoto (石己呂和居命)
Kamo-Hiko-no-Mikoto (鴨比古命)
Kamo-Hime-no-Mikoto (鴨比賣命) | shrine_Rank= {{ill|Shikigai-sha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigai-sha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=}}
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} Massha | founded= Before Enryaku 23 (804) | main_hall= [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] | alternative_names= | pilgrimage_sites= | annual_festival= | rituals= [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] }} '''Kamoshimo Shrine''' (鴨下神社, かもしもじんじゃ) is a **massha** (末社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (Inner Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. The **three enshrined deities** are considered **water deities** of the local region.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 59Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 68. == Overview == Kamoshimo Shrine is located in [[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki, Mie]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. It is one of **13 massha** of the **Inner Shrine (Naikū) located in Tamaki Town** and holds the **highest Rank** among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 13–14 Approximately **2.5 km away** from Kamoshimo Shrine is {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|ja|鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, an **Inner Shrine sessha** (摂社), which enshrines the **same deities** and shares a connection with Kamoshimo Shrine, as both are located in **Shirota-no-go (城田郷)**.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p. 200. The name **"Kamo"** was traditionally believed to derive from an **old place name**. However, with the excavation of the **Saikū Site (斎宮遺跡)**, a connection to the **Kamo clan (賀茂氏)**, who served as **water administrators (水司)**, has been proposed.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p. 199. The name **"Shimo" (下, "lower")** refers to its position at the **foot of a mountain**, in contrast to **Kamo Shrine**, which is located **midway up the mountain**.. The shrine grounds cover **3 tan, 9 se, 14 bu (approximately 3,914 m²)**Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p. 13, surrounded by **grape vineyards**. The shrine hall is built in **Shinmei-zukuri (神明造)** style with **ita-buki (板葺, plank roofing)**, enclosed by a **single-layered tamagaki fence (玉垣)**. There is **no offertory box (賽銭箱)**. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: - **Ishi-Korowake-no-Mikoto (石己呂和居命)** - **Kamo-Hiko-no-Mikoto (鴨比古命)** - **Kamo-Hime-no-Mikoto (鴨比賣命)** All three deities are considered **guardian deities of water management and irrigation in Katsuta (勝田)**. - **Ishi-Korowake-no-Mikoto** is the **child of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命)**, the **same deity enshrined at Kamo Shrine**. - **Kamo-Hiko-no-Mikoto and Kamo-Hime-no-Mikoto** are **male and female deities** whose names derive from the **common noun "Kamo" (鴨, "duck")**, symbolizing their dual nature. == History == Kamoshimo Shrine is a **Shikigai-sha (式外社)**, meaning it is **not listed in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō (延喜式神名帳)**. However, since it is classified as an **Ise Grand Shrine massha**, it must have existed before the **compilation of the Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō (皇大神宮儀式帳)**, that is, before **Enryaku 23 (804)**.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22 Like Kamo Shrine, Kamoshimo Shrine was **originally regulated to follow the same 20-year rebuilding cycle** (*shikinen sengū*, 神宮式年遷宮) as the **Ise Grand Shrine**, overseen by the **Zōkyōshi (造宮使)**. During **ancient times**, Kamoshimo Shrine was categorized as a **"Taisha" (田社, "Rice Paddy Shrine")**. However, it was the **only Taisha shrine** to receive such **special treatment**. However, due to the **political turmoil of the Sengoku period (戦国時代)**, the shrine fell into neglect and was left in **a state of disrepair**.Kaneko (1983): p. 61. In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was **restored** along with {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyoda Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}.Sakurai (1991): pp. 280–281. == Access == === Public Transportation === - **From Tamaki Station (田丸駅) on the JR Sangu Line (参宮線)** ** Approximately **24 minutes on foot (2.0 km southwest)** === By Car === - **From Tamaki IC (玉城IC) on the Ise Expressway (伊勢自動車道)** ** Take **Mie Prefectural Route 65 (三重県道65号度会玉城線, Sunny Road)** north for approximately **3 minutes (1.3 km)** ** **No designated parking lot available** == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * Ise Bunka-sha, ed. *O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage* (お伊勢さん125社めぐり), Ise Bunka-sha, December 23, 2008, 151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4. * Ujiyamada City Hall, ed. *Ujiyamada City History, Vol. 1* (宇治山田市史 上巻), Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, 1929, 862p. * Gakken Publishing, *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine* (伊勢神宮に行こう), Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by Sonoda Minoru, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1. * Kaneko, Nobuo. *Tamaki Town History, Vol. 1: The History and Legends of Southern Ise* (玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―), Mie Prefecture Historical Materials Series No. 61, Mie Prefecture Historical Society, August 10, 1983, 276p. * Sakurai, Katsunoshin. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine* (伊勢神宮の祖型と展開), Kokusho Publishing, November 30, 1991, 318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3. * Shikinaisha Research Group, ed. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6: Tōkaidō 1* (式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1), Kōgakkan University Press, February 28, 1990, 690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8. {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|鴨下神社|version=76118870|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Massha of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Kamoshimo Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:Buildings in Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikigeisha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[sn:Kamoshimo Shrine]] [[ja:鴨下神社]] [[sn:amoshimo Shrine]]

There is **no offertory box (賽銭箱)**. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: - **Ishi-Korowake-no-Mikoto (石己呂和居命)** - **Kamo-Hiko-no-Mikoto (鴨比古命)** - **Kamo-Hime-no-Mikoto (鴨比賣命)** All three deities are considered **guardian deities of water management and irrigation in Katsuta (勝田)**. - **Ishi-Korowake-no-Mikoto** is the **child of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命)**, the **same deity enshrined at Kamo Shrine**. - **Kamo-Hiko-no-Mikoto and Kamo-Hime-no-Mikoto** are **male and female deities** whose names derive from the **common noun "Kamo" (鴨, "duck")**, symbolizing their dual nature. == History == Kamoshimo Shrine is a **Shikigai-sha (式外社)**, meaning it is **not listed in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō (延喜式神名帳)**. However, since it is classified as an **Ise Grand Shrine massha**, it must have existed before the **compilation of the Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō (皇大神宮儀式帳)**, that is, before **Enryaku 23 (804)**.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22 Like Kamo Shrine, Kamoshimo Shrine was **originally regulated to follow the same 20-year rebuilding cycle** (*shikinen sengū*, 神宮式年遷宮) as the **Ise Grand Shrine**, overseen by the **Zōkyōshi (造宮使)**. During **ancient times**, Kamoshimo Shrine was categorized as a **"Taisha" (田社, "Rice Paddy Shrine")**. However, it was the **only Taisha shrine** to receive such **special treatment**. However, due to the **political turmoil of the Sengoku period (戦国時代)**, the shrine fell into neglect and was left in **a state of disrepair**.Kaneko (1983): p. 61. In **1874 (Meiji 7)**, the shrine was **restored** along with {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyoda Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}.Sakurai (1991): pp. 280–281. == Access == === Public Transportation === - **From Tamaki Station (田丸駅) on the JR Sangu Line (参宮線)** ** Approximately **24 minutes on foot (2.0 km southwest)** === By Car === - **From Tamaki IC (玉城IC) on the Ise Expressway (伊勢自動車道)** ** Take **Mie Prefectural Route 65 (三重県道65号度会玉城線, Sunny Road)** north for approximately **3 minutes (1.3 km)** ** **No designated parking lot available** == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * Ise Bunka-sha, ed. *O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage* (お伊勢さん125社めぐり), Ise Bunka-sha, December 23, 2008, 151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4. * Ujiyamada City Hall, ed. *Ujiyamada City History, Vol. 1* (宇治山田市史 上巻), Ujiyamada City Hall, January 20, 1929, 862p. * Gakken Publishing, *Let's Visit Ise Grand Shrine* (伊勢神宮に行こう), Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection Vol.1, supervised by Sonoda Minoru, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1. * Kaneko, Nobuo. *Tamaki Town History, Vol. 1: The History and Legends of Southern Ise* (玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―), Mie Prefecture Historical Materials Series No. 61, Mie Prefecture Historical Society, August 10, 1983, 276p. * Sakurai, Katsunoshin. *The Prototype and Development of Ise Grand Shrine* (伊勢神宮の祖型と展開), Kokusho Publishing, November 30, 1991, 318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3. * Shikinaisha Research Group, ed. *Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 6: Tōkaidō 1* (式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1), Kōgakkan University Press, February 28, 1990, 690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8. {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|鴨下神社|version=76118870|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Massha of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Kamoshimo Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:History of Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:Buildings in Tamaki, Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikigeisha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] }} [[sn:Kamoshimo Shrine]] [[ja:鴨下神社]] [[sn:amoshimo Shrine]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]]

{{Shrine | name= Kamoshimo Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image=[[File:Kamoshimo-jinja (Naiku) 03.jpg|270px|Kamoshimo Shrine]] | location = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District, Mie]], [[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki, Mie]] Katsuta Aza Uenoyama 3642 | latitude = 34 | lat_minutes = 28 | lat_seconds = 44.8 | longitude = 136 | lon_minutes = 37 | lon_seconds = 16.3 | map= Japan Mie | enshrined_deities= Ishi-Korowake-no-Mikoto (石己呂和居命)
Kamo-Hiko-no-Mikoto (鴨比古命)
Kamo-Hime-no-Mikoto (鴨比賣命) | shrine_Rank= {{ill|Shikigai-sha|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigai-sha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=}}
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} Massha | founded= Before Enryaku 23 (804) | main_hall= [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] | alternative_names= | pilgrimage_sites= | annual_festival= | rituals= [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] }} '''Kamoshimo Shrine''' (鴨下神社, かもしもじんじゃ) is a **massha** (末社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (Inner Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. The **three enshrined deities** are considered **water deities** of the local region.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 59Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 68. == Overview == Kamoshimo Shrine is located in [[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki, Mie]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. It is one of **13 massha** of the **Inner Shrine (Naikū) located in Tamaki Town** and holds the **highest Rank** among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 13–14 Approximately **2.5 km away** from Kamoshimo Shrine is {{ill|Kamo Shrine (Ise)|ja|鴨神社 (玉城町)|lt=Kamo Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, an **Inner Shrine sessha** (摂社), which enshrines the **same deities** and shares a connection with Kamoshimo Shrine, as both are located in **Shirota-no-go (城田郷)**.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p. 200. The name **"Kamo"** was traditionally believed to derive from an **old place name**. However, with the excavation of the **Saikū Site (斎宮遺跡)**, a connection to the **Kamo clan (賀茂氏)**, who served as **water administrators (水司)**, has been proposed.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p. 199. The name **"Shimo" (下, "lower")** refers to its position at the **foot of a mountain**, in contrast to **Kamo Shrine**, which is located **midway up the mountain**.. The shrine grounds cover **3 tan, 9 se, 14 bu (approximately 3,914 m²)**Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p. 13, surrounded by **grape vineyards**. However, since it is classified as an **Ise Grand Shrine massha**, it must have existed before the **compilation of the Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō (皇大神宮儀式帳)**, that is, before **Enryaku 23 (804)**.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 22 Like Kamo Shrine, Kamoshimo Shrine was **originally regulated to follow the same 20-year rebuilding cycle** (*shikinen sengū*, 神宮式年遷宮) as the **Ise Grand Shrine**, overseen by the **Zōkyōshi (造宮使)**.
